title: Small Business Transportation Investment Act of 2025
short_title: Small Business Transportation Investment Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill creates a three year test program that helps small transportation companies save money when buying vehicles. The companies can buy vehicles through the same system the government uses, which offers lower prices. To be part of the program, the companies must use these vehicles for transportation services for at least two years. When they are finished using the vehicles, they must donate some of them to nonprofit organizations.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Small Business Transportation Investment Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has learned the following facts. The General Services Administration runs a program that helps buy vehicles for federal agencies, state and local governments, and tribal organizations at good prices. If we let commercial transportation companies also use this vehicle buying program, it could make things work better, cost less money, and help these companies get newer, cleaner vehicles.

sections.title: Section 3 GSA Federal Fleet Pilot Program
sections.text: Within sixty days after this law is passed, the Administrator of General Services must start a test program. This program will let small transportation businesses buy vehicles through the same government buying system that federal agencies use. These small businesses will pay the same low prices and choose from the same vehicles as other groups that are already allowed to use this system, like certain government agencies and tribal organizations. However, each small business can only buy up to fifty vehicles per year. To join this program, small businesses must agree to several rules. They must use each vehicle they buy to provide transportation services for at least two years, unless the vehicle breaks down and cannot be fixed. If they sell a vehicle before two years are up, they must pay back the government the difference between what the vehicle is worth on the open market and what they originally paid. They also must donate at least one out of every five vehicles to a local nonprofit organization after they stop using the vehicle for their business. The Administrator must write reports to Congress about how the program is working. The first report is due twelve months after the law passes, and another report one year after that. These reports must include information about which businesses participated, how much money businesses saved, and how the program helped the environment by getting cleaner vehicles on the road. When the program ends, the Administrator must write a final report that says whether the program worked well and whether it should become permanent or be expanded. The Administrator can write any rules needed to run this program. The test program will end three years after this law is passed. In this section, Administrator means the Administrator of General Services. A covered small business means a small business that provides transportation services to people. Ground transportation service means driving passengers in cars, taxis, limousines, shuttles, buses, or other vehicles for money. This includes special transportation for people with disabilities and medical transportation that is not for emergencies. Non emergency medical transportation means providing rides for people who need help getting around. Small business has the same meaning as it does in the Small Business Act.
